meta.title: Plasma-Catalytic Ammonia Synthesis Beyond the Equilibrium Limit

claims.text: The ammonia synthesis reaction is exergonic at near-ambient conditions, with delta G(298 K) = -32.8 kJ/mol.
Typical industrial-scale NH3 synthesis processes operate at temperatures of 700-800 K and pressures of 100-200 atm.

location.quote:
synthesis processes thus operate at temperatures in the range of 700–800 K and pressures of
100–200 atm

claims.text: Ammonia synthesis can be performed at milder conditions (~473 K, 1 atm) when catalysts are operated alongside non-thermal plasmas.

location.quote:
ammonia synthesis can be performed at milder temperatures and pressures (∼473 K, 1 atm) when catalysts are operated alongside non-thermal plas-
mas

claims.text: Prior plasma-catalytic kinetic experiments in a differential flow DBD reactor showed elevated site-time yields of NH3 at atmospheric pressure and 438 K, as well as a shift in optimal catalyst material.

location.quote:
showed elevated site-time yields of NH3 at atmospheric pressure
and 438 K, as well as a shift in optimal catalyst material

claims.text: NH3 yields are predicted to exceed bulk thermodynamic equilibrium limits on materials that are thermal-rate-limited by N2 dissociation.

location.quote:
NH3 yields are predicted
to exceed bulk thermodynamic equilibrium limits on materials that are thermal-rate-limited by N2
dissociation.

claims.predicate: model_predicts NH3_pressure > equilibrium_NH3_pressure for weakly-N-binding materials

claims.text: The CSTR reactor model operates at atmospheric pressure and constant volume of 1 mL, with reactants introduced in stoichiometric ratio at a flow rate of 10 mL/min.

location.quote:
continuously stirred tank reactor (CSTR) operating at atmospheric pressure and constant
volume (1 mL). The reactants are introduced into the reactor in their stoichiometric ratio,
at a flow rate of 10 mL min−1.

claims.predicate: model_volume_mL == 1 and model_flow_rate_mL_min == 10 and P == atmospheric

claims.text: The model reactor is packed with catalyst having one type of active site (metal steps) with a total site density of 10^-3 mol/mL.

location.quote: with a total site density of 10−3 mol mL−1

claims.text: The intermediate N-binding catalyst (EN = -0.6 eV) sits at the top of the volcano curve and gives the highest NH3 yields of the three catalysts in Figure 2.

location.quote:
N with intermediate strength (EN = −0.6 eV), sits at the top of the volcano curve (see inset)
and provides the optimal balance between N2 activation and its subsequent hydrogenation.

claims.predicate: EN_intermediate_eV == -0.6 and intermediate_catalyst_yield > strong_catalyst_yield and > weak_catalyst_yield
claims.scope: Figure 2, thermal catalysis model, 473 K, 1 atm

claims.text: Three catalysts are modeled with strong (EN = -1.2 eV), intermediate (EN = -0.6 eV), and weak (EN = 0.0 eV) nitrogen binding energies.

location.quote:
characterized by strong (EN = −1.2 eV),
intermediate (EN = −0.6 eV), and weak (EN = 0.0 eV) nitrogen binding energies.

claims.text: The volcano curve inset in Figure 2 was calculated at 473 K, 1 atm, and 5% conversion of N2.

location.quote:
The inset shows the volcano curve for NH3 synthesis
calculated at 473 K, 1 atm, and 5% conversion of N2.

claims.text: Upon excitation, the N2 dissociation barrier is assumed reduced by 1 eV, corresponding to roughly three N2 vibrational quanta.

location.quote:
the N2 dissociation
barrier is reduced by 1 eV, corresponding to roughly three N2 vibrational quanta.

claims.text: DBD microdischarges are short-lived (~10 ns), followed by an afterglow period on the order of 1 microsecond.

location.quote:
electron impact reactions are only expected to occur during short-lived (∼ 10 ns) filamentary
microdischarges, followed by an afterglow period on the order of 1 µs

claims.text: For the optimal thermal catalyst, NH3 yields exceeding equilibrium are observed at kene = 10^-1 s^-1 in the temperature window 475-600 K.

location.quote:
NH3 yields
exceeding equilibrium for the optimal thermal catalyst are observed at kene = 10−1s
−1
, in
the temperature window of 475–600 K.

claims.predicate: optimal_catalyst beyond_eq at kene == 1e-1 s-1 over T_window_K == [475,600]

claims.text: Plasma-on models predict enhancements in NH3 over thermal catalysis for both materials if kene >= 10^-2 s^-1.

location.quote:
plasma-on models predict enhancements in NH3 produced over thermal catalysis for both
materials if the rate of plasma-activation of N2 is sufficiently high (kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
)

claims.text: For the weaker-binding material, NH3 pressures are predicted to exceed the equilibrium limit for kene >= 10^-2 s^-1.

location.quote:
NH3 pressures are
predicted to go beyond the equilibrium limit for kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
.

claims.text: In the experiments N2 and H2 were introduced into the reactor in a 2:1 ratio at a total flow rate of 40 SCCM.

location.quote:
We
introduced N2 and H2 into the reactor in a 2:1 ratio at a total flow rate of 40 SCCM.

claims.text: Background measurements were made in a reactor packed with 100 mg gamma-Al2O3.

location.quote:
These background measurements were made in a reactor packed with bare support material
(100 mg γ-Al2O3)

claims.text: The bulk reactor temperature was varied between 423-1123 K.

location.quote:
The bulk reactor temperature was varied between
423–1123 K.

claims.text: Reaction performance was evaluated at plasma powers of 5, 10, and 15 W, corresponding to specific energy inputs of 7.5, 15, and 22.5 kJ/L.

location.quote:
Reaction performance was evaluated at plasma powers of 5, 10, and 15 W,
corresponding to specific energy inputs of 7.5, 15, and 22.5 kJ L−1.

claims.predicate: plasma_powers_W == [5,10,15] and specific_energy_inputs_kJ_per_L == [7.5,15,22.5]; SEI == power_W*1000/flow_L_per_s consistency

claims.text: At all plasma powers, background NH3 yields maximized at about 920 K, after which they decreased.

location.quote:
At all plasma powers, NH3 yields maximized at about 920 K, after which they
decreased.

claims.text: Ni and Pt catalysts supported on gamma-Al2O3 were used at 100 mg material and 5 wt% metal.

location.quote: Ni and Pt catalysts supported on γ-Al2O3 (100 mg material, 5 wt% metal).

claims.text: The N-metal bond strength follows the order Ru > Ni > Pt; both Ni and Pt bind N more weakly than the optimal thermal catalyst Ru.

location.quote: the N-metal bond strength follows the order Ru > Ni > Pt

claims.text: Figure 5a plasma-catalytic NH3 yields on Ni and Pt were measured as a function of bulk gas temperature at 10 W plasma power.

location.quote:
we plot plasma-catalytic NH3 yields on these two catalysts as a function of
bulk gas temperature at 10 W plasma power.

claims.text: On the Pt catalyst, NH3 yields continued to increase with temperature, significantly exceeding the bulk equilibrium limit, before decreasing; Pt enhanced NH3 over background until about 1000 K.

location.quote:
Pt continued to enhance NH3 production over the background
at higher temperatures until about 1000 K, after which the yields converged.

claims.predicate: Pt_enhancement_until_T_K == ~1000 and Pt_yield > equilibrium_yield in equilibrium-limited regime

claims.text: Thermal NH3 decomposition experiments used a feed of 0.5% NH3, 9.5% He, balance 2:1 N2 and H2, at 40 mL/min inlet flow.

location.quote:
Inlet gas composition: 0.5%
NH3, 9.5% He, and balance of 2:1 N2 and H2.

claims.predicate: decomp_feed_pct == {NH3: 0.5, He: 9.5} and N2_H2_ratio == 2:1 and flow_mL_min == 40

claims.text: Of the tested materials Ni/Al2O3 is the most active for NH3 decomposition, lighting off at around 750 K, while Pt/Al2O3 and metal-free gamma-Al2O3 light off at approximately 900 K.

location.quote:
Ni/Al2O3 is the most active for NH3
decomposition, lighting off at around 750 K. The decomposition curves for Pt/γ-Al2O3 and
metal-free γ-Al2O3 are nearly identical, lighting off at approximately 900 K.

claims.text: The ammonia synthesis reaction was modeled with delta H(298 K) = -91.8 kJ/mol.

location.quote: The ammonia synthesis reaction was modeled with ∆H (298 K) = −91.8 kJ/mol.

claims.text: The Boltzmann equation was solved for an N2 plasma at a reduced electric field of 100 Td; ke typically ranges between 10^-14 and 10^-9 cm3/s, and ne is typically in the range 10^14-10^15 cm-3.

location.quote: for a N2 plasma at a reduced electric field of 100 Td

claims.text: The reactor consists of a quartz tube (5 mm internal diameter, 1 mm wall thickness); the discharge gap is 1.75 mm and the discharge volume is 1.07 cm3.

location.quote:
Within this setup, the discharge gap
is 1.75 mm, and the discharge volume is 1.07 cm3.

claims.predicate: tube_ID_mm == 5 and wall_mm == 1 and discharge_gap_mm == 1.75 and discharge_volume_cm3 == 1.07

claims.text: Catalyst particle sizes were determined by TEM based on at least 300 particles.

location.quote: All particle sizes were based on at least 300 particles.

claims.text: Catalysts were pretreated in 20 SCCM H2 at 773 K for 30 min followed by 30 min in 20 SCCM He at 773 K.

location.quote:
Catalysts were pretreated in 20 SCCM (standard cubic centimeter per minute) H2 at 773 K
for 30 min followed by 30 min in 20 SCCM He at 773 K

claims.text: Each data point is based on an average of three GC injections, with error bars corresponding to the standard deviation of these three measurements.

location.quote:
Each data point is based off
of an average of three injections by the GC and the error bars shown correspond to the
standard deviation of these three measurements.

claims.text: Beyond-equilibrium NH3 yields are observed experimentally and are greater on materials with weaker N binding (e.g. Pt) that are inactive for thermal ammonia synthesis.

location.quote:
NH3 yields exceeding the
bulk equilibrium limit are observed and are greater on materials with weaker N binding en-
ergies (e.g. Pt) that are inactive for thermal ammonia synthesis.

claims.text: Yields can be increased further past equilibrium by increasing plasma power, but re-equilibrate at high bulk temperatures where thermal pathways dominate.

location.quote:
with increasing
plasma power, ammonia yields extend further beyond the equilibrium limit, while yields
re-equilibrate at temperatures at which thermal chemistry becomes dominant.

claims.predicate: NH3_yield increases with plasma_power; reverts to equilibrium at high T
